name: dft-default
description: DFT: handoff gate → SSOT read → passthrough OR scan-insert via OpenROAD (+optional Fault ATPG) → report

tasks.detail:
Refuse to run on stale or missing netlist:
- [ ] <ip>/syn/out/synth.v exists and non-empty
- [ ] mtime(synth.v) >= max(mtime(<ip>/rtl/*.{sv,v}))
If missing: emit `[DFT HANDOFF MISSING] run /syn first` and STOP.
If stale: emit `[DFT STALE NETLIST]` and STOP.
Gate: handoff confirmed.

tasks.content: [DFT] Read SSOT dft: section; passthrough if disabled
tasks.detail:
Read <ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml `dft:` section.
- If `dft.enabled: false` (or section absent): copy synth.v → scan.v, write a passthrough scan_chains.json, emit `[DFT DISABLED] passthrough`, then jump to the report task.
- If enabled: extract scan_enable_port, max_chains, max_chain_length, scan_clock, atpg.* fields.
Gate: enabled flag known + parameters captured.

DFT does NOT synthesize a phantom port — RTL author owns it.
Grep synth.v for `(input|output)[^;]*\b<scan_enable_port>\b`.
If not present: STOP with `[DFT PORT MISMATCH] scan_enable_port '<X>' not declared in RTL`.
Gate: port confirmed in netlist.

- [ ] command -v openroad
- [ ] [ -r $SKY130_LIB ]
Gate: tool + PDK both green.

Run scripts/write_dft_tcl.py <ip>. tcl must contain:
- [ ] read_liberty $SKY130_LIB (absolute path)
- [ ] read_verilog <ip>/syn/out/synth.v
- [ ] link_design <top>
- [ ] set_dft_config -max_length N -max_chains M -clock_mixing no_mix
- [ ] preview_dft -verbose
- [ ] insert_dft
- [ ] write_verilog <ip>/dft/out/scan.v
- [ ] report_dft
Gate: run.tcl exists.

Run scripts/run_openroad_dft.py <ip>. If rc != 0: tail dft.log. Common errors:
- 'No registers found' → design is combinational (RTL has no FFs)
- 'Clock not found' → scan_clock SSOT field doesn't match a clock in synth.v
- 'Liberty has no scan cells' → wrong corner (only ss/ff/tt corners with scan flops)
Gate: openroad rc=0, scan.v non-empty, scan FF cells (sdfrtp_*) present in scan.v.

Run scripts/parse_chains.py <ip>.
schema: {top, tool, scan_chains[{id,length,scan_in,scan_out,clock,edge}], summary{total_ffs,ffs_in_chains,ffs_skipped,chains,max_length,min_length,scan_enable_port,mode}}.
Gate: scan_chains.json exists; summary.chains > 0; ffs_skipped == 0 (or has explicit reason).

Run scripts/run_fault_atpg.py <ip>. Best-effort:
- If `fault` not on PATH: warn and skip.
- If Fault returns coverage < target: emit `[DFT COVERAGE LOW]` warning but DO NOT fail.
Writes <ip>.test patterns + coverage.json.
Gate: either ATPG ran with coverage >= target, OR was skipped explicitly.

Run scripts/write_report.py <ip>.
Report must show:
- [ ] mode (passthrough | scan_insert | scan_insert+atpg)
- [ ] scan port summary + per-chain table
- [ ] coverage if Fault ran
- [ ] open-source gap notes (MBIST/JTAG/LBIST not inserted)
Gate: dft.report.md written.

Print one summary line:
`[DFT HANDOFF] <ip>/dft/out/scan.v ready (chains=N, scan_ffs=M) — run /pnr-fp`.
Note that PnR-fp will fall back to syn/out/synth.v if scan.v is absent — DFT is optional from PnR's perspective.
Gate: handoff line emitted.
